recurringPattern: Bottom tab bar with 4-5 icon+label tabs (Home, Search, Activity, Profile, More), a hamburger menu for additional items, and a top status bar that either hides or unnaturally persists. Active tab uses the brand's primary color fill.
designHarm: The 5-tab bottom bar is a sensible mobile pattern, but AI applies it universally regardless of app architecture. Apps with 3 or fewer primary destinations get unnecessary tabs. The 'More' overflow becomes a dumping ground for important features. The bottom bar competes with gesture-based navigation on modern iOS/Android.
legitimateExceptions: Content-consumption apps (Instagram, Twitter) where 5 primary destinations are genuine
Utility apps with well-defined, mutually exclusive sections
possibleFixtures: Top-tab navigation for secondary-level mobile sections
originalObservations: AI-generated mobile navigation always defaults to 5 bottom tabs regardless of app depth.
Tab icons are always outlined when inactive and filled when active, using the primary brand color.
The hamburger menu is always positioned top-left on screens that also have bottom tabs.
